 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-klux","title":"EPIC: Public Profiles & Reputation","description":"## Origin
Moved from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-61i.

## Overview
Public user profiles and author reputation system for the community.

## Public Profile Page (/user/[username])

### Profile Header
- Avatar
- Display name
- Username
- Bio
- Join date
- Social links
- Reputation badges

### Content Tabs
- Public Prompts
- Public Packs
- Public Skills
- Activity (recent public actions)

### Stats Display
- Total public prompts
- Total saves received
- Average rating
- Member since

## Reputation System

### Reputation Score
Calculated from:
- Number of public prompts (+10 each)
- Total saves received (+1 each)
- Positive ratings received (+2 each)
- Age of account
- Featured content (+50 each)

### Badges
- "New Member" - < 30 days
- "Contributor" - 5+ public prompts
- "Popular" - 100+ total saves
- "Top Rated" - Average rating > 90%
- "Featured Author" - Has featured content
- "Founding Member" - Early adopter

### Display
- Badge icons on profile
- Reputation score (optional to show)
- Badge tooltips with criteria

## Database
- Add to user_profiles: username (unique), bio, social_links, is_public
- user_badges (user_id, badge_type, awarded_at)
- Calculate reputation score via materialized view or on-demand

## Privacy
- Users can make profile private (content still public if visibility=public)
- Can hide reputation score
- Can choose which badges to display

-{"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-l1t7","title":"Typography Standardization: Eliminate arbitrary text sizes","description":"# Epic: Typography Standardization

## Background & Problem Statement

The UI overhaul established a 5-size typography scale in globals.css:
- text-xs (12px)
- text-sm (14px)
- text-base (16px)
- text-lg (18px)
- text-xl+ (headings)

However, several components still use arbitrary values like `text-[11px]` and `text-[10px]`, which:
1. Break the design system's rhythm
2. Are harder to maintain
3. Create subtle inconsistencies between similar elements

## Files Affected
1. PromptDetailModal.tsx - 3 instances of text-[11px]
2. SpotlightSearch.tsx - 1 instance of text-[11px], 3 instances of text-[10px]

## The Fix
Replace all arbitrary text sizes with the nearest standard size:
- `text-[11px]` → `text-xs` (12px, +1px - negligible visual difference)
- `text-[10px]` → `text-xs` (12px, +2px - slightly larger but more readable)

## Design Rationale
- 10-11px text is often too small for comfortable reading
- The extra 1-2px improves accessibility without breaking layouts
- Consistency trumps pixel-perfect sizing for this use case

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Zero instances of `text-[Npx]` in component files
- [ ] All text uses standard Tailwind classes
- [ ] No layout shifts from size changes
- [ ] Text remains readable at all sizes

## Dependencies
- Should be done alongside or after color fixes (often same lines of code)
- Low risk, can be batched with color changes

## Estimated Scope
- 2 files
- 7 class replacements
- Minimal testing required","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-12T00:37:31.250583614-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T00:37:31.250583614-05:00"}
+{"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-l1t7","title":"Typography Standardization: Eliminate arbitrary text sizes","description":"# Epic: Typography Standardization

## Background & Problem Statement

The UI overhaul established a 5-size typography scale in globals.css:
- text-xs (12px)
- text-sm (14px)
- text-base (16px)
- text-lg (18px)
- text-xl+ (headings)

However, several components still use arbitrary values like \`text-[11px]\` and \`text-[10px]\`, which:
1. Break the design system's rhythm
2. Are harder to maintain
3. Create subtle inconsistencies between similar elements

## Files Affected
1. PromptDetailModal.tsx - 3 instances of text-[11px]
2. SpotlightSearch.tsx - 1 instance of text-[11px], 3 instances of text-[10px]

## The Fix
Replace all arbitrary text sizes with the nearest standard size:
- \`text-[11px]\` → \`text-xs\` (12px, +1px - negligible visual difference)
- \`text-[10px]\` → \`text-xs\` (12px, +2px - slightly larger but more readable)

## Implementation Note
**Typography fixes are included in the color fix tasks:**
- g0oe (PromptDetailModal) - includes "Also Fix Typography" section
- usn5 (SpotlightSearch) - includes "Also Fix Typography" section

This keeps related changes together (same lines of code) and reduces PR churn.

## Design Rationale
- 10-11px text is often too small for comfortable reading
- The extra 1-2px improves accessibility without breaking layouts
- Consistency trumps pixel-perfect sizing for this use case

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Zero instances of \`text-[Npx]\` in component files
- [ ] All text uses standard Tailwind classes
- [ ] No layout shifts from size changes
- [ ] Text remains readable at all sizes

## Validation
The color validation tests (tzmc) include a typography consistency check that will verify no arbitrary text sizes remain.

## Dependencies
- Typography fixes are bundled into g0oe and usn5 tasks
- tzmc validates the result

## Estimated Scope
- 2 files (via g0oe and usn5)
- 7 class replacements
- Minimal testing required","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-12T00:37:31.250583614-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T01:27:01.282517492-05:00"}
 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-l35x","title":"E2E Tests: Admin Dashboard & Moderation","description":"## Origin
Moved from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-whb.

## Task
Create comprehensive E2E tests for the admin dashboard with detailed logging.

## Test Scenarios

### Admin Access Tests
1. **Access Control**
   - Non-admin user cannot access /admin
   - Admin user can access /admin
   - Admin role is verified on each request
   - Tampering with role fails

### User Management Tests
2. **User List**
   - List all users with pagination
   - Search users by email/name
   - Filter by tier (free/premium)
   - Filter by status (active/suspended/deleted)
   - Sort by join date, name, etc.

3. **User Detail**
   - View user profile details
   - View subscription history
   - View content (prompts, packs, skills)
   - View activity log

4. **User Actions**
   - Suspend user account
   - Unsuspend user account
   - Grant premium manually
   - Revoke premium
   - Delete user (soft delete)
   - Impersonate user (admin debug)

### Content Moderation Tests
5. **Reported Content**
   - View reported content queue
   - See report details and reason
   - View the reported content
   - Approve content (dismiss reports)
   - Remove content from Swap Meet
   - Warn content creator
   - Ban repeat offenders

6. **Featured Content**
   - Mark prompt as featured
   - Set "Prompt of the Day"
   - Manage featured carousel
   - Schedule featured content

### Analytics Tests
7. **Dashboard Metrics**
   - Total users (by tier)
   - New signups (daily/weekly/monthly)
   - Active users
   - Subscription revenue (MRR)
   - Churn rate
   - Content metrics (prompts, packs, skills)

8. **Export Data**
   - Export user list to CSV
   - Export revenue reports
   - Export content reports

### System Management Tests
9. **Announcements**
   - Create system announcement
   - Set announcement visibility
   - Schedule announcement
   - Dismiss announcement

10. **Maintenance Mode**
    - Enable maintenance mode
    - Users see maintenance page
    - Admins can still access
    - Disable maintenance mode

## Logging Requirements
- Log all admin actions with admin ID
- Log before/after state for user changes
- Log moderation decisions with reason
- Log impersonation sessions
- Screenshot admin dashboard states
- Log analytics query performance

## Test Infrastructure
- Create test admin user
- Create test regular users to manage
- Seed reported content for moderation
- Clean up test data after suite

## Files to Create
- `e2e/admin/access.spec.ts`
- `e2e/admin/user-management.spec.ts`
- `e2e/admin/content-moderation.spec.ts`
- `e2e/admin/analytics.spec.ts`
- `e2e/admin/system.spec.ts`
- `e2e/lib/admin-helpers.ts`

## Success Criteria
- Admin access properly restricted
- User management flows work
- Moderation actions apply correctly
- Analytics display accurate data
- Audit trail is complete

 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-l87c","title":"EPIC: Content Reporting & Moderation","description":"## Origin
Moved from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-5ly.

## Overview
Content reporting and moderation workflow for community safety.

## Report Flow (User Side)

### 1. Report Button
- Available on all public content (prompts, packs, skills)
- Icon button (flag) with "Report" tooltip
- Only for logged-in users
- Can't report own content

### 2. Report Dialog
- Reason selection:
  - Spam or misleading
  - Inappropriate content
  - Copyright violation
  - Other (with text field)
- Optional additional details
- Submit button

### 3. Confirmation
- "Report submitted" toast
- "We'll review this within 24 hours"

## Moderation Flow (Admin Side)

### 1. Moderation Queue
- List of pending reports
- Sort by: date, report count, severity
- Filter by: status, type, reason

### 2. Review Interface
- View reported content
- View reporter info and reason
- View report history for this content
- Actions:
  - Dismiss report (no action)
  - Warn author
  - Remove content
  - Ban user

### 3. Actions
- Remove: Set visibility to 'removed', notify author
- Warn: Send warning email, record strike
- Ban: Suspend account, notify user

## Database Schema
```typescript
export const contentReports = pgTable("content_reports", {
  id: uuid("id").primaryKey().defaultRandom(),
  reporterId: uuid("reporter_id").notNull(),
  contentType: text("content_type").notNull(),
  contentId: uuid("content_id").notNull(),
  reason: text("reason").notNull(),
  details: text("details"),
  status: text("status").default("pending"),  // pending, reviewed, actioned, dismissed
  reviewedBy: uuid("reviewed_by"),
  reviewedAt: timestamp("reviewed_at"),
  action: text("action"),  // none, warned, removed, banned
  createdAt: timestamp("created_at").defaultNow(),
});
```

-{"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-luzx","title":"Audit and fix BasketSidebar.tsx color palette","description":"# Task: Audit BasketSidebar.tsx for Color Consistency

## Context
The Color Consistency epic (s6ex) mentions BasketSidebar.tsx as potentially needing updates, but no audit was done. This task is to verify and fix any inconsistencies.

## Audit Checklist
Run grep to find any zinc/indigo usage:
\`\`\`bash
grep -n 'zinc-\|indigo-' apps/web/src/components/BasketSidebar.tsx
\`\`\`

## Expected Issues (to verify)
Based on similar components, likely locations:
- Background colors (sidebar panel)
- Border colors
- Text colors (headers, descriptions)
- Button hover states
- Empty state styling
- Divider lines

## Standard Patterns to Apply
From the already-fixed components:

### Backgrounds
\`\`\`tsx
// Panel background
bg-white dark:bg-neutral-900

// Muted/secondary backgrounds
bg-neutral-50 dark:bg-neutral-800/50
\`\`\`

### Borders
\`\`\`tsx
border-neutral-200 dark:border-neutral-800
\`\`\`

### Text
\`\`\`tsx
// Primary
text-neutral-900 dark:text-white

// Secondary
text-neutral-600 dark:text-neutral-400

// Muted
text-neutral-500 dark:text-neutral-500
\`\`\`

### Button Hover
\`\`\`tsx
hover:bg-neutral-100 dark:hover:bg-neutral-800
\`\`\`

## Verification
After fixes:
1. \`grep -n 'zinc-' apps/web/src/components/BasketSidebar.tsx\` should return nothing
2. \`grep -n 'indigo-' apps/web/src/components/BasketSidebar.tsx\` should return nothing (unless Pro feature)
3. Visual check in light and dark mode
4. WCAG AA contrast verification

## Files Modified
- apps/web/src/components/BasketSidebar.tsx

## Dependencies
- Part of Color Consistency epic (s6ex)
- Should be done alongside other color fixes
- Blocks color validation tests (tzmc)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-12T01:24:44.640954502-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T01:24:44.640954502-05:00"}

 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-lx09","title":"EPIC: Legal Pages & Compliance","description":"## Origin
Moved from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-ynx.

## Overview
Legal pages, compliance requirements, and user-facing policies.

## Required Pages

### 1. Terms of Service (/terms)
- User obligations
- Content ownership and licensing
- Subscription terms
- Limitation of liability
- Termination conditions

### 2. Privacy Policy (/privacy)
- Data we collect
- How we use it
- Third-party services (Stripe, Supabase, analytics)
- User rights (access, deletion, export)
- Cookie policy
- Contact information

### 3. Community Guidelines (/guidelines)
- Acceptable content for Swap Meet
- Prohibited content
- Enforcement actions
- Reporting process
- Appeal process

### 4. Cookie Consent (if required)
- Cookie banner component
- Cookie preferences modal
- Store consent in localStorage + backend

## GDPR Compliance

### Data Export
- API endpoint to generate data export
- Background job to compile all user data
- Email notification when ready
- Download link with expiration

### Account Deletion
- Soft delete with 30-day grace period
- Hard delete after grace period
- Cancel subscription on deletion request
- Delete all user content
- Anonymize analytics data

## Implementation
- MDX pages for legal content (easy to update)
- Version tracking for legal documents
- "Accept updated terms" flow when terms change

## Task
Create E2E tests for referral program functionality.

## Test Scenarios

1. User has referral code
2. Code is unique
3. Copy code works
4. Share links work

### Referral Flow

6. Referral tracked on signup
7. Extended trial granted
8. Referral shows as pending

### Conversion

10. Referral marked as converted
11. Referrer gets reward
12. Both notified

### Edge Cases
13. Self-referral prevented
14. Expired codes handled
15. Duplicate referral prevented
16. Code case-insensitive

- e2e/referral/flow.spec.ts
- e2e/referral/rewards.spec.ts
- e2e/lib/referral-helpers.ts

## Success Criteria

- Rewards applied correctly
- Fraud prevented

+{"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-q4dz","title":"Add E2E tests for basket functionality","description":"# Task: E2E Tests for Basket Functionality

## Goal
Create comprehensive E2E tests for the basket feature, including adding/removing items, export functionality, and the new basket button on PromptCard (after bnc3 is implemented).

## Background
The basket allows users to collect prompts for bulk operations (export as markdown, download as skills). After bnc3 adds a basket button to PromptCard, we need tests covering the full workflow.

## Test File: e2e/basket.spec.ts

```typescript
import { test, expect } from '@playwright/test';

test.describe('Basket Functionality', () => {
  
  test.beforeEach(async ({ page }) => {
    await page.goto('/');
    await page.waitForSelector('[data-testid="prompt-card"]');
    console.log('[SETUP] Page loaded');
  });

  test.describe('Adding Items', () => {
    test('add to basket via card button', async ({ page }) => {
      // Get initial basket count
      const basketBadge = page.locator('[data-testid="basket-count"]');
      const initialCount = await basketBadge.textContent() || '0';
      console.log(\`[BEFORE] Basket count: \${initialCount}\`);
      
      // Click basket button on first card
      const firstCard = page.locator('[data-testid="prompt-card"]').first();
      const basketBtn = firstCard.locator('button[aria-label*="basket"]');
      await basketBtn.click();
      
      // Verify count increased
      await expect(basketBadge).toHaveText((parseInt(initialCount) + 1).toString());
      console.log('[PASS] Basket count incremented');
      
      // Verify button state changed
      await expect(basketBtn).toHaveAttribute('aria-label', /already in basket/i);
      console.log('[PASS] Button state updated');
    });

    test('add to basket via modal', async ({ page }) => {
      // Open modal
      await page.click('[data-testid="prompt-card"] button:has-text("View")');
      await page.waitForSelector('[role="dialog"]');
      
      // Click add to basket in modal
      await page.click('[role="dialog"] button:has-text("Add to Basket")');
      
      // Verify toast appears
      await expect(page.locator('text=Added to basket')).toBeVisible();
      console.log('[PASS] Added via modal');
    });

    test('cannot add same item twice', async ({ page }) => {
      const firstCard = page.locator('[data-testid="prompt-card"]').first();
      const basketBtn = firstCard.locator('button[aria-label*="basket"]');
      
      // Add item
      await basketBtn.click();
      await page.waitForTimeout(100);
      
      // Try to click again - should be disabled or show already added
      const isDisabled = await basketBtn.isDisabled();
      const ariaLabel = await basketBtn.getAttribute('aria-label');
      
      expect(isDisabled || ariaLabel?.includes('already')).toBeTruthy();
      console.log('[PASS] Duplicate add prevented');
    });
  });

  test.describe('Basket Sidebar', () => {
    test('opens and shows items', async ({ page }) => {
      // Add an item first
      await page.click('[data-testid="prompt-card"] button[aria-label*="basket"]');
      
      // Open basket
      await page.click('[aria-label*="basket"][aria-label*="open"]');
      await page.waitForSelector('aside');
      
      // Verify item is shown
      const basketItem = page.locator('aside [data-testid="basket-item"]');
      await expect(basketItem).toHaveCount(1);
      console.log('[PASS] Basket shows added item');
    });

    test('remove item from basket', async ({ page }) => {
      // Add item and open basket
      await page.click('[data-testid="prompt-card"] button[aria-label*="basket"]');
      await page.click('[aria-label*="basket"][aria-label*="open"]');
      await page.waitForSelector('aside');
      
      // Remove item
      await page.click('aside button[aria-label*="Remove"]');
      
      // Verify empty state
      await expect(page.locator('text=Your basket is empty')).toBeVisible();
      console.log('[PASS] Item removed, empty state shown');
    });

    test('empty state shows Browse CTA', async ({ page }) => {
      // Open empty basket
      await page.click('[aria-label*="basket"]');
      await page.waitForSelector('aside');
      
      // Verify CTA button
      const browseBtn = page.locator('aside button:has-text("Browse prompts")');
      await expect(browseBtn).toBeVisible();
      
      // Click should close sidebar
      await browseBtn.click();
      await expect(page.locator('aside')).not.toBeVisible();
      console.log('[PASS] Empty state CTA works');
    });
  });

  test.describe('Export Functionality', () => {
    test('download as markdown', async ({ page }) => {
      // Add items
      await page.click('[data-testid="prompt-card"]:nth-child(1) button[aria-label*="basket"]');
      await page.click('[data-testid="prompt-card"]:nth-child(2) button[aria-label*="basket"]');
      
      // Open basket
      await page.click('[aria-label*="basket"][aria-label*="open"]');
      await page.waitForSelector('aside');
      
      // Start download
      const [download] = await Promise.all([
        page.waitForEvent('download'),
        page.click('button:has-text("Download as Markdown")'),
      ]);
      
      expect(download.suggestedFilename()).toMatch(/\.md$/);
      console.log(\`[PASS] Downloaded: \${download.suggestedFilename()}\`);
    });

    test('download as skills ZIP', async ({ page }) => {
      // Add item and open basket
      await page.click('[data-testid="prompt-card"] button[aria-label*="basket"]');
      await page.click('[aria-label*="basket"][aria-label*="open"]');
      
      const [download] = await Promise.all([
        page.waitForEvent('download'),
        page.click('button:has-text("Download as Skills")'),
      ]);
      
      expect(download.suggestedFilename()).toMatch(/\.zip$/);
      console.log(\`[PASS] Downloaded: \${download.suggestedFilename()}\`);
    });
  });

  test.describe('Keyboard Accessibility', () => {
    test('basket button is keyboard accessible', async ({ page }) => {
      // Tab to first card's basket button
      const firstCard = page.locator('[data-testid="prompt-card"]').first();
      const basketBtn = firstCard.locator('button[aria-label*="basket"]');
      
      await basketBtn.focus();
      await page.keyboard.press('Enter');
      
      // Verify added
      const badge = page.locator('[data-testid="basket-count"]');
      await expect(badge).toHaveText('1');
      console.log('[PASS] Keyboard activation works');
    });
  });

  test.describe('Mobile: Swipe Actions', () => {
    test.use({ viewport: { width: 375, height: 667 } });
    
    test('swipe right adds to basket', async ({ page }) => {
      const card = page.locator('[data-testid="prompt-card"]').first();
      const box = await card.boundingBox();
      
      if (box) {
        // Swipe right gesture
        await page.mouse.move(box.x + 50, box.y + box.height / 2);
        await page.mouse.down();
        await page.mouse.move(box.x + box.width - 50, box.y + box.height / 2, { steps: 10 });
        await page.mouse.up();
        
        await page.waitForTimeout(300);
        
        // Verify toast or badge update
        const badge = page.locator('[data-testid="basket-count"]');
        await expect(badge).toHaveText('1');
        console.log('[PASS] Swipe right added to basket');
      }
    });
  });
});
```

## Logging Format
- \`[SETUP]\` - Test initialization
- \`[BEFORE]\` / \`[AFTER]\` - State snapshots
- \`[PASS]\` - Assertion passed
- \`[FAIL]\` - Assertion failed (automatic)

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Add via card button works
- [ ] Add via modal works
- [ ] Duplicate prevention works
- [ ] Remove from basket works
- [ ] Empty state displays correctly
- [ ] Export as markdown works
- [ ] Export as skills ZIP works
- [ ] Keyboard accessible
- [ ] Mobile swipe works

## Files to Create
- e2e/basket.spec.ts

## Dependencies
- Depends on bnc3 (keyboard alternatives / basket button on card)
- Depends on maui (improved empty state)
- Should run after those implementations","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-12T01:25:42.267502036-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T01:25:42.267502036-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-q4dz","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-bnc3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-12T01:25:54.833775941-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-q4dz","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-maui","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-12T01:26:00.005080594-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-qbem","title":"Implement ratings API endpoints","description":"## Origin
Moved from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-3w1.

## Task
Implement API endpoints for ratings system.

## Endpoints

### POST /api/ratings
Create or update rating.

Request:
```json
{
  "contentType": "user_prompt",
  "contentId": "uuid",
  "value": 5  // 1 (thumbs down) or 5 (thumbs up)
}
```

Response:
- Updated rating stats for the content
- User's current rating

### DELETE /api/ratings/:contentType/:contentId
Remove user's rating.

### GET /api/ratings/:contentType/:contentId
Get rating stats for content.

Response:
```json
{
  "total": 150,
  "positive": 142,
  "negative": 8,
  "approvalRate": 94.7,
  "userRating": 5  // null if not rated
}
```

### GET /api/ratings/user
Get user's rating history.

Query params:
- contentType: filter by type
- page, limit

## Implementation Notes

### Rating Stats Update
On rating change:
1. Update or insert rating record
2. Recalculate rating_stats for content
3. Return updated stats

Use database transaction to ensure consistency.

### Rate Limiting
- Max 60 ratings per user per hour
- Prevent rating spam

### Edge Cases
- User rates same content twice (update, don't duplicate)
- Content deleted while rating (graceful failure)
- Rating own content (allowed but discouraged in UI)

 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-qg15","title":"Implement bundle size optimization and monitoring","description":"## Origin
Mirror from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-lppg. Keep both repos in sync.

## Task
Optimize JavaScript bundle size and set up monitoring.

## Why This Matters
- Large bundles = slow load
- Mobile users on slow networks
- Core Web Vitals impact
- SEO ranking factor

## Optimization Targets
- Initial bundle < 100KB gzipped
- Per-route chunks < 50KB
- Total JS < 300KB gzipped
- No unused dependencies

## Strategies

### 1. Code Splitting
```typescript
// Dynamic imports for routes
const AdminDashboard = dynamic(() => import('@/app/admin/dashboard'), {
  loading: () => <Skeleton />,
});

// Split heavy components
const Editor = dynamic(() => import('@/components/editor'), {
  ssr: false,
  loading: () => <EditorSkeleton />,
});
```

### 2. Tree Shaking
```typescript
// Import specific functions
import { format } from 'date-fns/format';  // Good
// import { format } from 'date-fns';       // Bad - imports all

// Import specific icons
import { Plus } from 'lucide-react';  // Good
// import * as Icons from 'lucide-react';  // Bad
```

### 3. Dependency Audit
```typescript
// package.json - analyze with bundlephobia
{
  "scripts": {
    "analyze": "ANALYZE=true next build",
    "bundle-size": "size-limit"
  }
}

// .size-limit.json
[
  {
    "path": ".next/static/chunks/*.js",
    "limit": "300 KB"
  }
]
```

### 4. Lazy Loading
```typescript
// Lazy load below-fold components
const Features = lazy(() => import('./features'));
const Testimonials = lazy(() => import('./testimonials'));

// Use Intersection Observer
function LazySection({ children }) {
  const [isVisible, setIsVisible] = useState(false);
  const ref = useRef();
  
  useEffect(() => {
    const observer = new IntersectionObserver(([entry]) => {
      if (entry.isIntersecting) setIsVisible(true);
    });
    observer.observe(ref.current);
    return () => observer.disconnect();
  }, []);
  
  return <div ref={ref}>{isVisible ? children : <Placeholder />}</div>;
}
```

## Monitoring

### Bundle Analysis Report
```typescript
// Generate report on build
// .github/workflows/bundle-size.yml
- name: Analyze bundle
  run: |
    npm run build
    npm run analyze -- --json > bundle-stats.json
    
- name: Comment PR with bundle size
  uses: actions/github-script@v6
  with:
    script: |
      const stats = require('./bundle-stats.json');
      // Compare with main branch
      // Comment on PR if increase > 5%
```

### Size Budget CI Check
```yaml
# Fail if bundle too large
- name: Check bundle size
  run: npx size-limit
```

## Optimization Checklist
- [ ] Remove unused dependencies
- [ ] Use dynamic imports for routes
- [ ] Lazy load heavy components
- [ ] Import specific functions only
- [ ] Use next/image for images
- [ ] Enable gzip/brotli compression
- [ ] Preload critical resources
- [ ] Defer non-critical scripts

## Files to Create
- .size-limit.json
- scripts/analyze-bundle.ts
- .github/workflows/bundle-size.yml
- next.config.js (optimization settings)

## Success Criteria
- Initial load < 100KB gzipped
- Lighthouse performance > 90
- No regression in CI
- Bundle report on each PR

-{"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-wi4g","title":"Add removable filter chips showing active filters","description":"# Task: Add Active Filter Chips with Remove Buttons

## Problem Statement
When users apply filters, the only indication is:
1. The pill button is highlighted (easy to miss if scrolled)
2. Text below results: "15 prompts for 'testing' with tags: unit-tests, automation"

Users can't:
- See at a glance what filters are active
- Remove individual filters without scrolling back
- Quickly understand why results are limited

## Current Behavior
```
Results Header
├── "Testing" (h2, if category selected)
├── "15 prompts" (p, count)
└── "with tags: unit-tests, automation" (p, inline text)
```

## Proposed Solution
Add a row of removable chips between filter controls and results:

```tsx
// ActiveFilterChips.tsx
interface ActiveFilterChipsProps {
  query: string;
  category: PromptCategory | null;
  tags: string[];
  onRemoveQuery: () => void;
  onRemoveCategory: () => void;
  onRemoveTag: (tag: string) => void;
  onClearAll: () => void;
}

export function ActiveFilterChips({
  query,
  category,
  tags,
  onRemoveQuery,
  onRemoveCategory,
  onRemoveTag,
  onClearAll,
}: ActiveFilterChipsProps) {
  const hasFilters = query || category || tags.length > 0;
  
  if (!hasFilters) return null;
  
  return (
    <div className="flex flex-wrap items-center gap-2 py-4 border-b border-neutral-200 dark:border-neutral-800">
      <span className="text-sm text-neutral-500 dark:text-neutral-400 mr-1">
        Active filters:
      </span>
      
      {/* Search query chip */}
      {query && (
        <FilterChip
          label={`"${query}"`}
          icon={<Search className="w-3 h-3" />}
          onRemove={onRemoveQuery}
        />
      )}
      
      {/* Category chip */}
      {category && (
        <FilterChip
          label={category}
          icon={<Folder className="w-3 h-3" />}
          onRemove={onRemoveCategory}
          className="capitalize"
        />
      )}
      
      {/* Tag chips */}
      {tags.map((tag) => (
        <FilterChip
          key={tag}
          label={tag}
          icon={<Tag className="w-3 h-3" />}
          onRemove={() => onRemoveTag(tag)}
        />
      ))}
      
      {/* Clear all */}
      <button
        onClick={onClearAll}
        className="ml-2 text-xs text-neutral-500 hover:text-neutral-900 dark:hover:text-white transition-colors"
      >
        Clear all
      </button>
    </div>
  );
}

// Individual chip component
function FilterChip({ 
  label, 
  icon, 
  onRemove,
  className 
}: { 
  label: string; 
  icon?: React.ReactNode;
  onRemove: () => void;
  className?: string;
}) {
  return (
    <span
      className={cn(
        "inline-flex items-center gap-1.5 px-2.5 py-1 rounded-full",
        "bg-neutral-100 dark:bg-neutral-800 text-sm text-neutral-700 dark:text-neutral-300",
        "group transition-colors hover:bg-neutral-200 dark:hover:bg-neutral-700",
        className
      )}
    >
      {icon && <span className="text-neutral-400 dark:text-neutral-500">{icon}</span>}
      <span className="max-w-[150px] truncate">{label}</span>
      <button
        onClick={onRemove}
        className="ml-0.5 p-0.5 rounded-full text-neutral-400 hover:text-neutral-600 dark:hover:text-neutral-200 hover:bg-neutral-300 dark:hover:bg-neutral-600 transition-colors"
        aria-label={`Remove ${label} filter`}
      >
        <X className="w-3 h-3" />
      </button>
    </span>
  );
}
```

## Integration in page.tsx

```tsx
{/* Filter Controls */}
<div className="mb-4 space-y-4">
  <CategoryFilter ... />
  <TagFilter ... />
</div>

{/* Active Filter Chips */}
<ActiveFilterChips
  query={filters.query}
  category={filters.category}
  tags={filters.tags}
  onRemoveQuery={() => setQuery("")}
  onRemoveCategory={() => setCategory(null)}
  onRemoveTag={(tag) => setTags(filters.tags.filter(t => t !== tag))}
  onClearAll={clearFilters}
/>

{/* Results */}
<div className="mt-6">
  <h2>...</h2>
  <PromptGrid ... />
</div>
```

## Design Details
- **Chip style**: Rounded pill with subtle background
- **Icon per type**: Search icon for query, folder for category, tag for tags
- **Remove button**: X on right side, larger hit target on hover
- **Truncation**: Long values truncate with ellipsis
- **"Clear all"**: Text link at end, not a chip

## Accessibility
- Each chip's X button has aria-label: "Remove {filter} filter"
- Chips are focusable via keyboard
- Screen reader: "Active filters: search 'testing', category debugging, tag unit-tests"

## Animation (Optional)
- Chips animate in/out when added/removed
- Use Framer Motion AnimatePresence

## Testing
1. Apply search query → chip appears with query text
2. Apply category → chip appears with category name
3. Apply multiple tags → multiple tag chips appear
4. Click X on chip → that filter removed, results update
5. Click "Clear all" → all filters removed
6. Keyboard: Tab through chips, Enter to remove
7. Screen reader: chips announced correctly

## Files to Create/Modify
- NEW: apps/web/src/components/ActiveFilterChips.tsx
- MODIFY: apps/web/src/app/page.tsx

+{"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-wi4g","title":"Add removable filter chips showing active filters","description":"# Task: Add Active Filter Chips with Remove Buttons

## Problem Statement
When users apply filters, the only indication is:
1. The pill button is highlighted (easy to miss if scrolled)
2. Text below results: "15 prompts for 'testing' with tags: unit-tests, automation"

Users can't:
- See at a glance what filters are active
- Remove individual filters without scrolling back
- Quickly understand why results are limited

## Current Behavior
```
Results Header
├── "Testing" (h2, if category selected)
├── "15 prompts" (p, count)
└── "with tags: unit-tests, automation" (p, inline text)
```

## Proposed Solution
Add a row of removable chips between filter controls and results:

```tsx
// ActiveFilterChips.tsx
interface ActiveFilterChipsProps {
  query: string;
  category: PromptCategory | null;
  tags: string[];
  onRemoveQuery: () => void;
  onRemoveCategory: () => void;
  onRemoveTag: (tag: string) => void;
  onClearAll: () => void;
}

export function ActiveFilterChips({
  query,
  category,
  tags,
  onRemoveQuery,
  onRemoveCategory,
  onRemoveTag,
  onClearAll,
}: ActiveFilterChipsProps) {
  const hasFilters = query || category || tags.length > 0;
  
  if (!hasFilters) return null;
  
  return (
    <div className="flex flex-wrap items-center gap-2 py-4 border-b border-neutral-200 dark:border-neutral-800">
      <span className="text-sm text-neutral-500 dark:text-neutral-400 mr-1">
        Active filters:
      </span>
      
      {/* Search query chip */}
      {query && (
        <FilterChip
          label={`"${query}"`}
          icon={<Search className="w-3 h-3" />}
          onRemove={onRemoveQuery}
        />
      )}
      
      {/* Category chip */}
      {category && (
        <FilterChip
          label={category}
          icon={<Folder className="w-3 h-3" />}
          onRemove={onRemoveCategory}
          className="capitalize"
        />
      )}
      
      {/* Tag chips */}
      {tags.map((tag) => (
        <FilterChip
          key={tag}
          label={tag}
          icon={<Tag className="w-3 h-3" />}
          onRemove={() => onRemoveTag(tag)}
        />
      ))}
      
      {/* Clear all */}
      <button
        onClick={onClearAll}
        className="ml-2 text-xs text-neutral-500 hover:text-neutral-900 dark:hover:text-white transition-colors"
      >
        Clear all
      </button>
    </div>
  );
}

// Individual chip component
function FilterChip({ 
  label, 
  icon, 
  onRemove,
  className 
}: { 
  label: string; 
  icon?: React.ReactNode;
  onRemove: () => void;
  className?: string;
}) {
  return (
    <span
      className={cn(
        "inline-flex items-center gap-1.5 px-2.5 py-1 rounded-full",
        "bg-neutral-100 dark:bg-neutral-800 text-sm text-neutral-700 dark:text-neutral-300",
        "group transition-colors hover:bg-neutral-200 dark:hover:bg-neutral-700",
        className
      )}
    >
      {icon && <span className="text-neutral-400 dark:text-neutral-500">{icon}</span>}
      <span className="max-w-[150px] truncate">{label}</span>
      <button
        onClick={onRemove}
        className="ml-0.5 p-0.5 rounded-full text-neutral-400 hover:text-neutral-600 dark:hover:text-neutral-200 hover:bg-neutral-300 dark:hover:bg-neutral-600 transition-colors"
        aria-label={`Remove ${label} filter`}
      >
        <X className="w-3 h-3" />
      </button>
    </span>
  );
}
```

 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-wlg3","title":"CLI: Add collections command for premium users","description":"## Task
Add a collections command to the jfp CLI for premium users to browse and use their collections.

## Commands
- jfp collections - List user's collections
- jfp collections <name> - Show prompts in collection
- jfp collections <name> --export - Export collection to files
- jfp collections create <name> - Create new collection (syncs to premium)
- jfp collections add <collection> <prompt-id> - Add prompt to collection

## Integration Points
- Premium API: /api/cli/collections (from premium project)
- Requires authentication

## Output Formats
- Default: Pretty table with prompt counts
- --json: JSON output for scripting
- --export: SKILL.md files or markdown

## Success Criteria
- List and browse collections
- Export collections locally
- Create and modify collections (syncs to cloud)
- Works with synced local cache

## Cross-Repo Integration
